description_long: This object depicts a cluster of mid-rise apartment buildings arranged on a square plot, resembling a small urban neighborhood. The buildings vary in height, with the tallest structures centrally located and surrounded by shorter blocks. The facades are marked by regularly spaced windows, and the overall layout suggests a planned residential complex. The surface appears to be made of concrete or similar materials, with a muted, grayish tone. The arrangement includes open spaces between the buildings, possibly representing courtyards or parking areas. The design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function over form.
description: A group of mid-rise, gray apartment buildings with regular window patterns, arranged on a square plot in a compact urban layout.
description_view_0: From this angle, the central cluster of taller buildings is visible, with shorter blocks and open spaces on the right side.
description_view_1: This view highlights the front-facing facades of the buildings, showing the main entrances and the symmetrical arrangement of the structures.
description_view_2: The left side reveals more open space and the side profiles of the buildings, with the tallest structures towards the back.
description_view_3: From the rear, the alignment of the buildings and the separation between blocks are more apparent, with visible open areas on both sides.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular, grid-like pattern with even spacing and alignment across all building facades.
